metadata.objective: Create a realistic plan for classes, assignments, study, and project work

summary: Plan an academic week is slowed mainly by search the lms and email for deadlines, rewrite assignments into a task list, build the weekly calendar. The redesign adds repeatable structure while preserving human judgment.

diagnosis.description: Search the LMS and email for deadlines
painPoints: Information is scattered and deadlines get missed
diagnosis.recommendation: Use the same location, naming rule, and sequence every time.

diagnosis.description: Rewrite assignments into a task list
diagnosis.recommendation: Start from a reusable template instead of recreating the output.
Keep the decision with the user and provide a review checklist.
Keep the decision with the user and provide a review checklist.

priorityImprovements.description: Search the LMS and email for deadlines
painPoints: Information is scattered and deadlines get missed
priorityImprovements.recommendation: Use the same location, naming rule, and sequence every time.

priorityImprovements.description: Rewrite assignments into a task list
priorityImprovements.recommendation: Start from a reusable template instead of recreating the output.
Keep the decision with the user and provide a review checklist.

improvedWorkflow.title: Collect classes, commitments, and deadlines
improvedWorkflow.input: Course pages, email, calendar, and unfinished work
improvedWorkflow.action: Review each approved source once and record every dated commitment in the weekly intake.
improvedWorkflow.aiInvolvement: AI is not needed for collection; source links remain visible.
improvedWorkflow.humanReview: The student confirms dates against official course sources.
improvedWorkflow.failureCondition: Stop if a deadline is ambiguous and verify it with the instructor or syllabus.

improvedWorkflow.title: Identify assignments
improvedWorkflow.action: Separate assessed work, preparation, administration, and personal commitments.
improvedWorkflow.aiInvolvement: AI may group entries but cannot invent requirements.
improvedWorkflow.humanReview: Confirm every assignment against its official instructions.
improvedWorkflow.failureCondition: Stop if requirements or submission dates are missing.

improvedWorkflow.title: Break large assignments into smaller tasks
improvedWorkflow.action: Use the decomposition template to create actions that can be completed in one work block.
improvedWorkflow.aiInvolvement: AI may draft a decomposition from supplied instructions.
improvedWorkflow.humanReview: The student verifies that every rubric requirement is covered.
improvedWorkflow.failureCondition: Stop if the assignment instructions have not been provided.

improvedWorkflow.title: Estimate effort
improvedWorkflow.input: Actionable task list and prior experience
improvedWorkflow.action: Assign a low, expected, and high estimate to each task.
improvedWorkflow.aiInvolvement: AI may suggest a starting range; the student owns the estimate.
improvedWorkflow.humanReview: Adjust estimates for personal pace and current capacity.
improvedWorkflow.failureCondition: Stop if a task is still too vague to estimate.

improvedWorkflow.title: Identify dependencies
improvedWorkflow.action: Mark tasks that require another task, person, resource, or fixed event first.
improvedWorkflow.aiInvolvement: AI may flag language that suggests dependencies.
improvedWorkflow.humanReview: Confirm external dependencies and lead times.
improvedWorkflow.failureCondition: Stop if a prerequisite cannot be scheduled or obtained.

improvedWorkflow.title: Prioritize work
improvedWorkflow.input: Deadlines, effort ranges, dependencies, and course priorities
improvedWorkflow.action: Rank tasks by deadline risk, importance, dependency impact, and effort.
improvedWorkflow.aiInvolvement: AI may calculate a transparent first-pass order.
improvedWorkflow.humanReview: The student makes the final priority decisions.
improvedWorkflow.failureCondition: Stop if two priorities conflict and seek course guidance when needed.

improvedWorkflow.title: Create a realistic weekly schedule
improvedWorkflow.input: Prioritized task list and fixed commitments
improvedWorkflow.action: Place focused blocks with buffers, breaks, and no more than the available weekly capacity.
improvedWorkflow.aiInvolvement: AI may propose a draft schedule without accessing private calendars.
improvedWorkflow.humanReview: The student checks energy, accessibility needs, and feasibility.
improvedWorkflow.failureCondition: Stop if planned work exceeds available capacity.

improvedWorkflow.title: Create daily starting actions
improvedWorkflow.action: Write one small, concrete starting action for each scheduled work block.
improvedWorkflow.aiInvolvement: AI may rewrite vague tasks into concrete starting actions.
improvedWorkflow.humanReview: Confirm each action matches the actual assignment.
improvedWorkflow.failureCondition: Stop if an action cannot be started with available materials.

improvedWorkflow.title: Review unfinished work
improvedWorkflow.action: Use the missed-task recovery process to reschedule, reduce, renegotiate, or remove unfinished work.
improvedWorkflow.aiInvolvement: AI may present recovery options; it cannot make academic or health decisions.
improvedWorkflow.humanReview: The student chooses a realistic recovery action.
improvedWorkflow.failureCondition: Escalate to an instructor or support service when a deadline cannot be met.

improvedWorkflow.title: Update the following week
improvedWorkflow.action: Carry forward only active work and update future effort estimates with actuals.
improvedWorkflow.aiInvolvement: AI may summarize timing differences from non-sensitive records.
improvedWorkflow.humanReview: Confirm that completed or obsolete work is not carried forward.
improvedWorkflow.failureCondition: Stop if the source of a carried-forward task is unclear.

assets.name: Weekly intake
assets.content:
Week of:
Fixed commitments:
Official deadlines with source links:
Unfinished work:
Available work blocks:

assets.name: Assignment decomposition
assets.content:
Assignment:
Definition of done:
Rubric requirements:
Tasks (30–90 minutes each):
Dependencies:
First visible action:

assets.name: Effort estimation
assets.content: Task | Low estimate | Expected estimate | High estimate | Actual | Learning for next estimate

assets.name: Priority framework
assets.content:
- Deadline risk
- Academic importance
- Dependency impact
- Estimated effort
- Consequence of delay

assets.name: Weekly plan
assets.content: Day | Fixed commitments | Focus block | Buffer | Daily starting action

assets.name: Daily kickoff
assets.content:
- Open the required source
- Read the task definition
- Start the named first action
- Set the planned stop point
- Record status before switching

assets.name: Missed-task recovery
assets.content: Choose one: reschedule with a buffer; reduce scope within requirements; ask for clarification; renegotiate when appropriate; remove obsolete work. Never silently overload the next day.

assets.name: End-of-week review
assets.content:
Completed:
Unfinished and why:
Actual vs expected effort:
Deadline risks:
One planning change for next week:

assets.name: Weekly plan quality review
assets.content:
- Every deadline traces to an official source
- Large assignments have concrete next actions
- Dependencies are scheduled first
- Planned work fits available capacity
- Buffers and recovery time are present
- Private information is excluded from unapproved tools

assets.name: Planning first-pass instruction
assets.content: Using only the supplied assignment instructions, deadlines, fixed commitments, and available work blocks, draft a weekly plan. Show source dates, dependencies, effort ranges, buffers, and one daily starting action. Mark missing or conflicting information as [VERIFY]. Do not infer health, disability, or private circumstances. Return a draft for student review.

exampleExecution.scenario: Plan a week containing two classes, one assignment milestone, and an existing personal commitment.
observations: Illustrative run: nine official deadlines and commitments were captured with source links.
One assignment was split into four work blocks, and the first draft schedule exceeded capacity by 90 minutes before student review.
Illustrative total: 84 minutes, compared with the 100-minute user estimate; this is an example, not a measured product claim.
The student made all priority and capacity decisions and added a recovery block.

riskNotes: Review AI-assisted output for accuracy before use.
Do not include private health, disability, or account information in unapproved AI tools.

qualityIndicators: Human review finds no critical errors
The user can repeat the workflow without assistance
reviewQuestions: How many minutes did each step and the full workflow take?
How many factual, structural, or scheduling corrections were required?
Where did you stop, become uncertain, or need operator help?
Rate the effort required from 1 (very low) to 5 (very high).
Could you repeat the workflow next time without assistance? Why or why not?
Which reusable asset helped, and what should change before the next run?
